expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: heatmap highlighting effect scenarios cover activation, persistence, and reset behavior
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly enumerates all three focus areas under “Heatmap highlighting scenarios that must appear in Phase 4a”: activation (e.g., “Highlight activation via single tap on a cell”, “...tapping a different cell switches highlight target”), persistence (e.g., “Highlight persistence when interacting outside the heatmap”, “Highlight persistence through scroll/resize/viewport changes”), and reset (e.g., “Highlight reset by explicit clear action”, “...when switching visualizations / navigating away”, “...when filters or selectors change the underlying heatmap data”).

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: result.md targets “Phase 4a (Subcategory-only) coverage check” and states Phase 4a requirements: “subcategory-only QA draft... central topic → subcategory → scenario → atomic action chain → observable verification leaves”. The provided plan is structured accordingly (e.g., “Heatmap highlight effect (iOS)” subcategory with scenario bullets, step chains like “Open... → Tap... → [observable verifications]”), and it avoids top-layer canonical categories (also reinforced by the note: “do not use top-layer buckets like ‘EndToEnd’ or ‘Compatibility’ in Phase 4a”).
